    • Ingrid R.
      Glendora, CA
      322
      76
      0
      Mar 29, 2026
      Updated review

      My husband and I recently discovered this place and I am obsessed. Have been here so many times in a span of a couple weeks. Their arepas are amazing. I've had many of them now but my fave hands down is the chicken breast. It is so so flavorful and the green sauce on the side is so yummy. Great coffee as well. Service is quick and friendly and arepas are made to order. Fair prices too. Haven't tried the pastries yet but they look great. Just hard to order anything else when I know how good the arepas are!

      Mar 14, 2026Previous review

      Great coffee, chill ambiance, and delicious food. LOVE the arepas, especially the chicken one. Has a great neighborhood feel and welcoming staff. Highly recommend!

    • Hanan M.
      Victorville, CA
      81
      7
      6
      Apr 13, 2026
      4 photos

      This used to be my favorite spot for arepas, but after today's experience, I won't be coming back.

      I called ahead and clearly explained my order: three arepa orders, with one having the masa scooped out. I also specifically asked about the price and was told it would be $13 per order, consistent with what's listed online. However, when I arrived, I was told the orders would be charged separately, and the employee became visibly frustrated when I asked for clarification and requested that everything be grouped as discussed.

      What made the situation worse was the pricing. I was charged $15 per order, which didn't match what I was told over the phone, nor what's listed online or even on Yelp (where it shows around $10). When I politely questioned the discrepancy, the employee became argumentative and made the interaction uncomfortable.

      I paid, because that's not the issue--but being treated dismissively and feeling misled is. Pricing should be consistent and clearly communicated, and customers shouldn't feel like they're being taken advantage of for asking reasonable questions.

      Really disappointing experience from a place I used to enjoy.

    • Carl N.
      Elite 26All-Star
      Los Angeles, CA
      358 Coffee reviews
      Jul 12, 2025
      8 photos

      Have you ever wished for a nice cold coffee drink on a hot day or a warm arepas? Well you've come to the right cafe.

      Cafe Bolívar (est 2001) is a cute corner South American coffee shop on Ocean Avenue in Santa Monica. They have good selection of coffee & tea drinks, selective pastries, salads, breakfast, and special arepas. This cafe has a calming atmosphere with background music that's not too loud, and the staff are skilled. I came on a sunny warm afternoon to grab a cold drink/light lunch. I had a Cold Brew and Chicken Arepas. I enjoyed their refreshing cold brew coffee drink but so much on the pollo arepas. Its chicken was dry; arepas outside was crispy and crunchy but the inside was not moist. Moreover, their size was bloody small.

      Cafe's interior was naturally bright and aesthetically decorated. Indoor/outdoor seats were comfortable. Prices of the drinks were fair, however, arepas seem expensive. Service was on point. Staff were cordial. Free wifi. Plenty of street parking.

      Overall, it's a nice coffee shop.

      See all photos from Carl N. for bolivar coffee + arepa bar

      I have been a long time fan and customer of Cafe Bolivar. Sadly an unauthorized extra charge was charged on my Cash App as a tip. I had already given a generous cash tip after I ordered. I will go there tomorrow to speak with them. Very disappointing.

    • Iris G.
      Elite 26
      Downey, CA
      27 Coffee reviews
      Aug 28, 2025
      1 photo

      I stumbled onto this place because I was looking for a good cup of coffee and something light to eat. Located at a corner you can see that there is plenty of seating available indoors and outdoors. By the time I got there, 10 a.m. the place was full and there was a line.

      Took me a good 8 min from the time I arrived to the time I got to the counter to order. To my surprise it was only 3 people managing the cafe. A female who was fully dedicated to making the arepas. An older gentleman who was busy making the coffee and taking orders and finally another female chopping background chopping ingredients.

      I ordered the Bolívar latte and the an order of arepas. Each order comes with two at $15. I got the mozzarella with chorizo arepa as well as the chicken arepa. Took a good 10 minutes for the arepas to come to the table. Both were very delicious and fresh. I don't know if the arepas normally take this long to make, but if they do ....trust me it's worth the wait. Honestly the arepas were so good I forgot to take a picture :( I devoured them so fast I don't even think I was breathing lol.

      Bolivar latte comes with condensed milk is 10/10.
      Both arepas .. 10/10.

      Bolivar latte comes with condensed milk

    • Carlo G.
      Elite 26All-Star
      Los Angeles, CA
      172 Coffee reviews
      Apr 17, 2025
      1 photo

      Good experience for an early and light dinner at this Venezuelan café!
       
      I came in around 5pm on a Tuesday, and the café was relatively busy. The interior is nice, very comforting and a good place to catch up with friends or study.
       
      I got two arepas for $14, one was mozzarella with chorizo and another one was pulled flank steak. I thought both were good. The arepa itself was crispy on the outside and soft on the inside. The fillings were just okay though, I wish there was more flavor. The flank steak one lacked steak.
       
      Parking in the area can get a bit difficult, so I just walked over from my work building. I believe there is street parking nearby.
       
      Overall, a good experience and I would be down to come again to try their drinks.

      Chorizo and flank steak arepas

    • Winn H.
      Elite 26All-Star
      Koreatown, Los Angeles, CA
      237 Coffee reviews
      Dec 22, 2024
      3 photos

      Tip: Park on at the park on 17th St X Ocean Park Blvd if possible.

      - - - - - -

      bolivar cofee + arepa bar was a nice little coffee spot. Upon walking in, the general stand with a petite menu is present for a quick flip through. As usual, i went with an iced latte to taste their beans, and I would not recommend this as an evening drink. Their beans were bold and flavorful; you will be kept awake!

      Speaking of petite, they do have a restroom, and the door was measured to perfection, sliding out but never hidden an adjacent door that leads outside in the back. There are plenty of seating options with power outlets and fast Wi-Fi. My only complaint was that parking was annoying (as expected in LA) Nearby street parking wasn't the easiest despite it being a weekday night. The nearby park (on 17th St X Ocean Park Blvd) does have parking options, but have a gander at the parking signs - classic LA experience, right?!

    • Elizabeth S.
      CA, CA
      5 Coffee reviews
      Aug 20, 2025
      2 photos

      Love this little cafe that feels like a hidden gem! I've been there twice now and tried their pulled pork arepas and mango arepa. The pork ones come with a house salsa that brightens up the arepa and the mango one comes with a generous slice of mango and cheese making for a delicious sweet/ salty combination. They look small but they're packed with flavor and are filling. They do have a minimum 2 order which makes for a nice meal, or a good snack if you're sharing with a friend.

      It's not crazy busy when I've been in there. Pretty quite and easy going atmosphere where you can sit and chat for a while with friends. There's typically people working on laptops but there's not too many options for outlets and I don't think they have Wi-Fi.

      It does take some time for the arepas to come out but it's worth the wait for fresh arepas. I like that they're open until 7 which is a bit later than most cafes in the area!

      Classic Caesar Salad

      2 Pulled Pork Arepas

    • Rachel B.
      Santa Monica, CA
      308
      12
      3
      Jul 1, 2025
      1 photo

      I've loved this place for years. It's my absolute favorite place to get a legitimate Venezuelan bar. The Pacifico with mango and cheese is phenomenal... But I love to try them all. The coffee drinks are solid as well, and it's such a lovely place to work And hang out.
      I wish they made more cafés like this, but I'm glad this one is still around!

      Cafe Bolivar Pacifico Sandwich

      Functional and analytics cookies

      (required)

      Functional cookies allow the website to function and enable us to provide enhanced features and personalisation. Analytics cookies allow us to understand how visitors use our site and to measure and optimize the site.

      Targeting cookies

      These cookies are intended to make advertising more relevant to users and more valuable to advertisers. For example, we may use Cookies to serve you interest-based ads. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising, but you will still see ads.
